content.problemStatement: Rectify the error in the code.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two integers a and b separated by a space.
content.outputFormat: For each test case print a single line containing the value of a/b rounded to one decimal place. If b is zero print -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each line shows a divided by b rounded to one decimal place.
7 divided by 2 equals 3.5, already has one decimal digit.
Both divisions result in 0.5, which is printed with one decimal place.

solution.approach: For each test case we only need to compute a / b as a floating point number and round it to one decimal place using the standard half‑up rule. If b is zero the division is undefined, so we output -1. The rounding can be done by formatting the double with "%.1f" (Locale.US) which follows half‑up rounding. The algorithm runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.
